| Config Path         | `OPIK_CONFIG_PATH`           | `OPIK_CONFIG_PATH`      | Custom config file location                    |

| Flush Timeout       | `OPIK_DEFAULT_FLUSH_TIMEOUT` | N/A                     | Default flush timeout (Python only)            |
| TLS Certificate     | `OPIK_CHECK_TLS_CERTIFICATE` | N/A                     | Check TLS certificates (Python only)           |
| Batch Delay         | N/A                          | `OPIK_BATCH_DELAY_MS`   | Batching delay in ms (TypeScript only)         |
| environment        | `OPIK_ENVIRONMENT`      | Default environment tag for traces - Optional                        |
| batchDelayMs       | `OPIK_BATCH_DELAY_MS`   | Batching delay in milliseconds - Defaults to `300`                   |
| holdUntilFlush     | `OPIK_HOLD_UNTIL_FLUSH` | Hold data until manual flush - Defaults to `false`                   |
+| trackDisable       | `OPIK_TRACK_DISABLE`    | Disable tracing of traces and spans - Defaults to `false`            |

+  // When tracking is disabled, the SDK never sends data, so backend
+  // credentials are not required. Skip the cloud credential checks so an
+  // instrumented app can run without an API key or a local deployment.
+ * Runtime state for whether tracing is active.
+ * The instrumentation layer (the `track` decorator and integrations) consults
+ * this before creating traces/spans. The default is derived once from the
+ * `trackDisable` config (`OPIK_TRACK_DISABLE` env var / config file) and cached,
+ * so the hot path never re-reads the environment or config file. A runtime
+ * override set via `setTracingActive` takes precedence over the config default
+      // If the config can't be resolved, fall back to active without caching
+      // so a later, well-formed read can still derive the real default.
+ * Enable or disable tracing at runtime. Overrides the `trackDisable` config
+ * Whether tracing is currently active. Returns the runtime override if one was
+ * Clear any runtime override so the `trackDisable` config default applies again.
